{"society_power": 8.2, "state_power": 7.4, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": 0.1, "key_event": "RTI, rights-based laws, and anti-corruption mass mobilization", "reasoning": "Society surged via RTI/MGNREGA-style entitlements, a vibrant 24-hour media, mobile/internet coordination, and mass anti-corruption and anti-rape movements that institutionalized into new parties. The state's fiscal/coercive capacity grew modestly (nuclear deal standing, NIA, 2014 majority) but was offset by corruption scandals, service-delivery gaps, and Maoist contestation, yielding a classic Red Queen balance with only slight net state gain."}
